normalization: Claim-bearing sections were extracted from the page's SSR MDContent and rewritten as bounded factual paraphrases; the full provider page is not retained.
rights_note: This capture keeps only short claim-level paraphrases and structural locators so the repository does not redistribute the provider document.

items.normalized_evidence: The guide distinguishes reference generation from editing or extending a target video and warns that generic reference phrasing can cause the operation to be classified incorrectly.
The BytePlus examples display a spaced prompt-visible handle such as at-sign Image 1; this is evidence for those BytePlus examples, not a universal cross-surface grammar.
The guide recommends a small purposeful set of references and cautions that automatically using the full allowance can create priority, style, and subject-identification conflicts.
For a character, the guide favors a clean facial reference plus a separate full-body reference and warns that multi-view collage references can confuse subject identity.
After defining a referenced subject, the guide recommends repeating one stable, unambiguous subject label whenever that subject appears.
The guide recommends ordering events with shot labels and cautions against forcing rigid duration limits for every segment when natural event pacing is more important.
The guide recommends one camera-movement type per shot because combining several primary moves can increase visual instability.

notes: Research on 2026-07-11 recorded the same raw MDContent hash as the prior 2026-07-10 observation. The raw provider bytes are not redistributed; reviewers must re-fetch the pinned URL and match the recorded hash and locators.
